https://www.behance.net/sadlycreative.aiReasoning: Role-fit summary: Junior level with primary focus on Brand Identity. Strengths: Polished visual execution across various mockups; Consistent application of the primary logo and aesthetic; Technical proficiency in using design tools for mockups Gaps: Complete absence of strategic thinking, problem statement, or design rationale; No explicit presentation of a comprehensive brand identity system (e.g., logo variations, full color palette with rationale, typographic hierarchy); Visual language is generic and lacks distinctiveness or originality for the category To reach next level: To reach a Score 4, the designer must articulate the brand strategy, present a comprehensive brand system with rationale, and demonstrate a more distinctive visual language that moves beyond generic aesthetics. (Based on 3/3 analyzed projects)

https://www.behance.net/sheetal-thapa.aiReasoning: Role-fit summary: Mid level with primary focus on Brand Identity. Strengths: Clear visual system demonstrated through logo construction, color palette, and typography.; Cohesive and sophisticated aesthetic applied consistently across various touchpoints (poster, business card, digital, pattern).; Evidence of intentional design decisions, particularly in the logo's conceptual breakdown. Gaps: Lack of explicit written brand strategy, problem statement, or design rationale beyond visual cues.; Absence of brand voice and tone guidelines, which are crucial for a complete brand system.; The 'why' behind specific stylistic choices is visually implied but not articulated in text. To reach next level: To reach a Score 5, the project needs to explicitly articulate the brand strategy, problem statement, and brand voice/tone, demonstrating deeper strategic thinking beyond just visual execution. (Based on 3/3 analyzed projects)
